title: Reflect live state into demo3 sliders and displays
summary: 0.6.39 makes the attached demo3 controls reflect live player state so progress and volume sliders move with playback and the current and total displays stop feeling blind.
changes: The attached demo3 progress slider now reflects the live normalized playback position on the existing player refresh cadence.
The attached demo3 volume slider now reflects the live player volume.
The attached demo3 current and total text displays now show current time and total duration for video, or current index and count for image playlists.
The attached demo3 shuffle control label now shows Shuffle On or Shuffle Off.
reasoning: The player had enough runtime truth already, but demo3 was not consuming it, which made the scene feel opaque during testing.
This stays in the attached runtime sync seam instead of inventing a new state channel or hot loop.
The state update is cached per attached screen so it stays cheap.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: The demo3 scene should now show live progress and volume state instead of making scrubbing feel blind.
The current and total readouts should update with playback and playlist state.
The shuffle control should now expose state in its label.
knownIssues: This slice does not yet fix the left and right control world-space placement overlap.
This slice does not yet add folder and filename displays or remaining-time formatting.
